purpose: Resolve ACT-060 by checking whether core-ops should be treated as a direct entrypoint, a profile-first entrypoint, or a substrate/delegator before installable adapter manifests exist.

codex.network_note: Model-route calls completed after transient WebSocket certificate/reconnect errors in the local network path.

scenarios.staging: Temporary CODEX_HOME with preview core-ops plus the normal shared user skill root visible.
scenarios.interpretation: When mature owner skills are visible and no profile-first instruction is given, Codex routes the maintenance task directly to leaf/router skills rather than to core-ops.

scenarios.staging: Temporary CODEX_HOME with preview skills plus the normal shared user skill root visible.
scenarios.prompt_condition: The prompt explicitly requires profile-first selection before leaf skills.
scenarios.interpretation: Codex can use core-ops as the profile-level entrypoint when the runtime or prompt enforces a profile-first selection step.

scenarios.staging: Temporary HOME without the shared user skill root plus temporary CODEX_HOME containing only the preview core-ops skill and built-in system skills.
scenarios.interpretation: Codex selects core-ops directly when it is the only installed operational profile surface.

decision.delegation_policy: Core-ops should be selected in isolated or profile-first contexts, but should not be forced to override mature owner skills when the full shared skill root is visible.
decision.installable_manifest_gate: Installable adapters may proceed only if manifests preserve profile-first or isolated-profile activation semantics and document full-root delegation to mature owner skills.

next_actions: Encode selection_semantics in skill-kernel adapter metadata.
Prototype installable adapter manifests after the selection_semantics field is validated.
